Ibrahim is called Khalil-ullah, the close friend of Allah. He smashed the idols of his people and stood alone for tawhid.
Nimrod threw him into a great fire, but Allah commanded the fire to be cool and safe for him.
He was tested with the command to sacrifice his son Ismail; both submitted, and Allah ransomed Ismail with a great ram.
He and Ismail built the Ka'bah in Makkah and called people to Hajj.
